Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses 1/2 ounce every day. Over the course of a leap year, there are 366 days. To find out how many ounces of shampoo she needs, we multiply 1/2 ounce per day by 366 days, resulting in 183 ounces of shampoo needed. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, we need to determine how many bottles are required to cover 183 ounces. This calculation shows that she needs [x] bottles, where [x] is the number of bottles required.

[amount] bottles are needed to cover the required amount of shampoo. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so [amount] bottles will provide the necessary amount of shampoo for the leap year.
